text.title: One Task → One Branch → One Worktree → One Agent (Runtime-Isolated) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Harness spawns N worktrees but they all boot servers on port 4000. Only the first works; subsequent agents fail boot and retry-storm.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed worktrees isolate runtime. They don't.
antipattern.fix: Allocate port ranges per worktree centrally; assert no overlap at spawn.

antipattern.symptom: Two worktrees run migrations against the same DB. Schema half-applied; both fail.
antipattern.root-cause: DB namespace not parameterized per worktree.
antipattern.fix: Inject `APP_DB_NAMESPACE` env per worktree; reject empty values.

antipattern.symptom: File manifest is `**/*`. Agent edits every file; merge conflicts at every PR.
antipattern.fix: Reject wildcards in manifest; require explicit path list with a max length (e.g., ≤30 paths).
